concerns.text: Task T024 and T025 in Phase 5 (US3) duplicate the IDs of T024 and T025 in Phase 4 (US2). This creates a fatal ambiguity in the execution order and dependency graph. The US3 test tasks (Contract test for attribution schema, Integration test for IoU) must be re-indexed (e.g., T038, T039) to ensure unique identification and correct parallel-safe grouping.

Task T026 in Phase 5 (US3) duplicates the ID of T026 in Phase 4 (US2). T026 in Phase 4 is 'Add logging for training progress', while T026 in Phase 5 is 'Implement InputXGrad'. This ID collision breaks the deterministic ordering check and makes it impossible to distinguish the producer (logging) from the consumer (attribution) in the dependency graph.

Task T027, T028, T029, T030 in Phase 5 follow the ID collision of T026. The entire US3 implementation block (T026-T030) is misnumbered, overlapping with US2 tasks. This violates the 'Producer before consumer' semantic layer because the unique IDs required to track the flow from 'Model Training' (US2) to 'Attribution' (US3) are corrupted.

Task T031 is marked [P] (parallel-safe) but depends on the completion of the synthetic generator logic in T005 and the metadata schema in T006. While T031 modifies the same file as T005, marking it [P] implies it can run concurrently with T005, which is a hidden shared-state violation. It should be sequential or explicitly noted as a dependent extension.
